chapter.label: Tale 31
chapter.title: The Conversion And Life Of Mary The Harlot

paragraphs.text: Two elders set off from Agaion to Tarsos in Cilicia. By the providence of God, they came to an inn where they could rest, for the heat was intense. There they found three younger men who had a harlot with them going to Agaion. The three elders sat discreetly apart; one of them took the holy gospel out of his travelling-bag and began to read aloud. When the harlot who was with the youths saw the elder begin to read, she came and sal down near hirn, forsaking the youths. The elder drove her off, saying to her: ‘Wretched woman, you seein very indecent. Are you not ashamed to come and sit near us? In reply she answered: ‘Oh father, please do not treat me with loathing. Even if I am filled with every kind of sin, the master of all, our Lord and God, did oot send away the harlot who came to iim.’ The elder answered her: ‘But that harlot remained a harlot no longer’. Ske said to him: ‘My hope is in the Son of the living God that from this day forward neither will I continue in this sin’. Forsaking the youths and everything she had, she followed the elders. They placed her in a women’s monastery called Nakkiba, near Agaion. I saw her as an old woman of great
experience. It was from her that I heard all this and her name was Mary.
